source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Hydra-Racker Collision

event.top_event: Structural damage to Hydra-Racker and Top-Drive systems
event.incident_type: Equipment failure due to human error
actions_taken: Incident documented and reported to BSEE
event.summary: On August 7, 2014 at 0525 hours during pipe tripping operations, the Assistant Driller retracted the Hydra-Racker with elevators still closed and top drive extended. Without communicating with the Auxiliary Driller, the Assistant Driller lowered the top drive with drill pipe still connected to elevators and block. The top drive struck the drill pipe, causing it to bow and damage multiple top drive components. The drill pipe broke out of the Hydra-Racker heads, shearing mounting bolts and causing a 500 lb lower guide arm to fall 12 feet to the rig floor. The incident occurred in the last 30 minutes of the last shift prior to crew change when employees were not focused on the task.
key_phrases: Poor communication between Assistant Driller and Auxiliary Driller
No visual confirmation and verbal communication
Crew fatigue near end of shift

hazards.name: Pressurized drill pipe and equipment under load
Heavy suspended loads (Hydra-Racker, top drive, drill pipe)
Mechanical equipment with moving parts (top drive, elevators, Hydra-Racker)

threats.name: Loss of communication between drill crew members during pipe handling
Operator error in sequencing top drive and Hydra-Racker operations
Failure to follow Job Safety Analysis (JSA) procedure for pipe tripping
Reduced situational awareness and focus near end of shift

consequences.name: Structural damage to Hydra-Racker heads and mounting bolts
Dropped object hazard - 500 lb lower guide arm falling 12 feet
Potential personnel injury from falling equipment
Operational downtime and equipment repair costs ($49,497)

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) procedure for pipe tripping operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure safe sequencing of operations
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to prevent unsafe operational sequences

supporting_text: Operators did not follow JSA procedure for tripping pipe out of the hole

controls.name: Verbal communication and coordination between Assistant Driller and Auxiliary Driller
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure safe communication during coordinated operations
controls.lod_basis: Communication protocol to prevent operational conflicts

supporting_text: Poor communication between the Assistant Driller and the Auxiliary Driller
Without communicating with the Auxiliary Driller the Assistant Driller then lowered the top drive
There was no visual confirmation and verbal communication between the Auxiliary Driller and Assistant Driller

controls.name: Visual confirmation during pipe handling operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to verify safe positioning of equipment before operations
controls.lod_basis: Visual verification to prevent collision between top drive and drill pipe

supporting_text: There was no visual confirmation and verbal communication between the Auxiliary Driller and Assistant Driller

controls.name: Main Driller supervision and operation of top drive
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure qualified operator controls critical equipment
controls.lod_basis: Supervisory control to prevent unauthorized or unsafe equipment operation

supporting_text: Main Driller was not operating the top drive at the time of the accident

controls.name: Crew alertness and focus during critical operations
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to maintain operator situational awareness
controls.lod_basis: Human factors control to prevent errors due to fatigue and reduced focus

supporting_text: The incident happened within the last 30 minutes of the last shift prior to crew change; employees were not focused on the task at hand
Crew works a 28 day on and 28 day off rotation

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
